question.text: The Black-Scholes model requires which five inputs?

answer.text: Spot price, Strike price, Volatility, Time to expiry, Risk-free interest rate
Spot price, Forward price, Delta, Gamma, Theta
Spot price, Strike price, Volume, Open interest, Margin
Spot price, Strike price, Dividend yield, GDP growth rate, Inflation rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Black-Scholes uses: (1) Spot price (S), (2) Strike price (X), (3) Volatility (σ), (4) Time to expiry (t), (5) Risk-free rate (r). These five factors determine the option premium. **The Trap:** The Greeks (Delta, Gamma etc.) are OUTPUTS of the model, not inputs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Black-Scholes inputs are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Indian exchanges use the Black-Scholes model for currency option theoretical price computation.
